articles.title: 'Using Lebanon as bargaining chip': President Joseph Aoun accuses Iran of interference
articles.description: In an interview broadcast by CNN on Friday, Aoun addressed Iran's Revolutionary Guards directly, declaring, "It's not your country, it's our country... It's not your job to interfere into our country."

articles.title: Spanish Proverb of the Day: “A young woman is to an old man the... — Life lessons on desire, temptation, self-control, and why impulsive choices often lead to unintended consequences
articles.description: Spanish Proverb of the Day highlights a traditional saying that reflects historical attitudes toward age, gender roles, and moral interpretation in older cultural expressions. The proverb “A young woman is to an old man the horse that he rides to hell” is a metaphorical statement that uses extreme imagery to express caution about desire, temptation, and relationships with significant age differences.

articles.title: Karnataka CM change reflects growing public anger against Congress misrule: PM Modi
articles.description: PM Modi: Karnataka CM change reflects public anger in Karnataka as PM Narendra Modi criticises Congress at Surat event highlighting infrastructure projects and development push in Gujarat and across India after Karnataka leadership change remarks PM

articles.title: Pressure on Modi over Russia ties to fail: Putin
articles.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin asserts that US pressure on India concerning its relationship with Moscow is futile. He emphasizes that Russia does not perceive India's expanding cooperation with Washington and other nations as a threat to their bilateral ties. Putin expressed confidence in the future of India-Russia economic relations, projecting bilateral trade to reach $100 billion.

articles.title: Odisha chief secretary asks districts to ensure public participation in statewide outreach programme
articles.description: Odisha chief secretary asks districts to ensure public participation in statewide outreach programme

articles.title: Woman alleges death threats after filing harassment plaint
articles.description: Woman alleges death threats after filing harassment plaint

articles.title: Japan PM Takaichi may visit next month
articles.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi may visit India in July for an annual summit. This visit aims to boost cooperation in security and economic matters. Discussions will focus on China's increasing influence and energy security. The trip's dates depend on Japan's parliamentary schedule. Takaichi might also visit Assam. This would be her first visit to India as Prime Minister.

articles.title: Minister Ramalinga Reddy quits DK Shivakumar cabinet within two days over portfolio row
articles.description: Karnataka's political landscape has shifted dramatically with the resignation of minister Ramalinga Reddy from the cabinet. His departure, which came before he officially took charge of his portfolio, stems from his disappointment over not being awarded the Greater Bengaluru Development position. This incident has created a stir, leading the administration of DK Shivakumar into a challenging predicament.

articles.title: PMK holds multi party meeting demanding caste survey
articles.description: PMK chief Anbumani Ramadoss urges Tamil Nadu caste survey at multi-party meet, citing updated socio-economic data and defending 69% reservation policy.

articles.title: Speaking the local language: Inside Annamalai’s break from BJP
articles.description: TVK Vijay: Annamalai's exit from BJP signals shifting Tamil Nadu politics as TVK Vijay reshapes opposition space amid Dravidian party challenges and growing debate on national parties' relevance in the state landscape today in Tamil Nadu politics now

articles.title: Karmelo Anthony Trial: No Black jurors in murder trial? What happened during jury selection
articles.description: Murder trial of Karmelo Anthony in fatal stabbing case attracts national attention for lack of Black jurors.

articles.title: TMC to challenge Speaker's decision naming Ritabrata Banerjee as LoP in Calcutta HC
articles.description: The decision was taken at a meeting convened by TMC chairperson and West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee at her residence

articles.title: LoP & Order: TMC Faction Fight In Bengal Reveals Why 'Leader Of Opposition' Post Is So Coveted
articles.description: When political factions fracture, control over this single office can determine which group achieves formal legitimacy and who is relegated to political obscurity

articles.title: Raichur district completes 85.70% mapping so far
articles.description: Raichur district has successfully mapped 85.70% of eligible voters ahead of the 2025 electoral rolls revision.

articles.title: TMC To Move Court Over Recognition Of Bengal LoP, Kalyan Banerjee Targets Ritabrata Banerjee
articles.description: TMC will move court Monday challenging recognition of the Leader of the Opposition in the West Bengal Assembly, calling it legally untenable and escalating TMC BJP tensions.

articles.title: India’s Cockroach Party fights attempts to discredit the movement
articles.description: On this edition of Access Asia, we explore how India’s nascent Cockroach Janta Party has tapped into the frustrations of the country’s youth. We speak with the party’s spokesperson, Saurav Das, who tells us that the movement is demanding accountability from the government. Political scientist Christophe Jaffrelot also weighs in on why young people in India are disillusioned and what the country’s opposition parties can learn from the viral movement.

articles.title: Vijayanagara launches Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls; draft list on August 5
articles.description: Vijayanagara initiates Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls; draft list to be published on August 5.

articles.title: Audience Questions 'Hostility to Dissent' in India at CJI's London Lecture - Vid...
articles.description: A moderator had to intervene during a public lecture given by Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant at the Birkbeck College of the University of London after one of the members of the audience posed a question on dissent being allegedly suppressed in India.

articles.title: Putin Signals Openness To Peace Deal As Zelensky Pushes For Direct Talks
articles.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Moscow remains open to a peace agreement with Ukraine if both sides can reach acceptable terms. The remarks came after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy published an open letter calling for direct talks and proposing a face-to-face meeting to end the war. The Kremlin confirmed Putin is aware of the proposal, while the Russian leader also said proposals backed by U.S. President Donald Trump could form the basis of a future settlement. Whether a Putin-Zelensky meeting takes place remains uncertain as both sides continue to differ on key issues.

articles.title: IRGC Takes On Two US Warships At Once 'Racing Towards' Iran Coast; Missiles Fired, Troops On Alert
articles.description: A fresh maritime confrontation has emerged in the Gulf of Oman, with Iran claiming its navy challenged two U.S. Navy destroyers operating near Iranian waters. According to Iranian state media, warning shots were fired and missiles and drones were deployed during the encounter, prompting the American vessels to change course. Tehran also claimed that the USS Tripoli amphibious assault ship left the area. Iranian officials warned that longer-range missiles could be used if U.S. ships return. The United States has not confirmed the incident and has not publicly responded to the claims. The reports come amid continuing tensions following the recent conflict involving Iran, the United States, and Israel, as diplomatic efforts and military signaling continue to unfold simultaneously across the region.

articles.title: Watch: U.S.- Iran war: Peace talks stall after Hezbollah rejects truce
articles.description: Peace talks between Lebanon and Israel stall as Hezbollah rejects a conditional truce, demanding a comprehensive ceasefire and full withdrawal.

articles.title: Six-day search for American student in Japan’s Kyoto continues after argument with mother over ChatGPT use
articles.description: Six-day search continues in Kyoto for 20-year-old American student James 'Weston' Higginbotham, who walked off after a vacation argument with his mother over ChatGPT.

articles.title: RS Polls: Rahul Gandhi, Kharge Arrive In Bengaluru For AICC Chief’s Nomination Amid K’taka Cong Crisis
articles.description: Bengaluru: Amid the crisis faced by the new Congress government led by Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar, Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha, Rahul

articles.title: China ready to work with Myanmar to maintain border stability: Wang Yi
articles.description: China expresses readiness to collaborate with Myanmar for border stability and the safety of Chinese personnel, says Wang Yi.

articles.title: K Annamalai: From IPS 'Singham' To BJP's Biggest Face In Tamil Nadu- What Changed?
articles.description: K Annamalai's journey is one of the most fascinating stories in Indian politics. Born into a farming family in Tamil Nadu, he studied engineering, earned an MBA from IIM Lucknow, and joined the Indian Police Service in 2011. Known as the 'Singham' of Karnataka Police, he built a reputation as a fearless officer before resigning to join the BJP in 2020. Within a year, he became the youngest BJP state president in Tamil Nadu and emerged as the party's most recognisable face in the state. But as political equations changed, so did his role. Here's the story of K Annamalai's rise, challenges, and transformation.

articles.title: After exit from BJP, Annamalai launches movement, eyes new party for 2031 polls
articles.description: Annamalai says he conveyed his decision to quit BJP to its leadership months ago, admits long-running differences with the party, especially on Tamil Nadu issues

articles.title: MWCD Internship programme 2026: Applications open for women students, teachers, social activists; check eligibility and key details
articles.description: The Ministry of Women and Child Development is inviting applications for its August-September 2026 internship. This two-month program offers women students, scholars, teachers, and activists from rural and non-tier 1 cities a chance to experience policy-making and government initiatives. Participants will observe program functions, contribute to policy work, and research critical issues, gaining valuable exposure to national governance.

articles.title: Assam: 12 legislators take oath as ministers in Himanta Biswa Sarma cabinet; strength boosts to 17
articles.description: As many as 12 legislators who took as ministers on Friday as Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma expanded his cabinet, taking the strength of the council of ministers to 17.

articles.title: 'Not your country': Lebanese leaders rebuke Iran as Israel, Hezbollah trade attacks
articles.description: Lebanon's president and prime minister criticised Iran on Friday for opposing the latest ceasefire deal between the Lebanese government and Israel, saying their country should not be used by Tehran as a "bargaining chip" in talks with Washington. Despite the truce, Lebanese media reported continued Israeli attacks on the south, while Iran-backed Hezbollah said it targeted Israeli troops with drones and rockets.

articles.title: The phone call that exposed cracks in the Trump-Netanyahu bond
articles.description: A recent heated phone call between US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has exposed public strains. Trump reportedly called Netanyahu 'fucking crazy' over Israeli threats to strike Beirut. This incident has political ramifications for Netanyahu ahead of Israeli elections. While both leaders have had disagreements, they remain allies.

articles.title: Government doctors protest across Telangana over transfer process, allege violations of GO 38
articles.description: Government doctors in Telangana protest against transfer process violations of G.O. 38, ensuring ongoing healthcare services remain unaffected.

articles.title: Annamalai resigns from BJP, Nitin Nabin accepts resignation
articles.description: On Tuesday, Annamalai met Home Minister Amit Shah, BJP National President Nitin Nabin and senior leader BL Santosh.

articles.description: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has begun his journey to India ahead of a planned protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

articles.description: The court was hearing a couple’s challenge to the refusal of local authorities to add the man’s name to the birth certificate of his elder daughter, currently aged 13, who was born through IVF before the couple married.

articles.title: CJP asks supporters to skip airport gathering to meet Abhijeet Dipke ahead of its first 'peaceful' Delhi protest
articles.description: On Friday, CJP spokesperson asserted that the planned protest will be peaceful and within the framework of law.

articles.title: Three arrested: Police forms SIT in alleged conversion, assault case of Kolkata woman
articles.description: Police have also appealed to members of the public to come forward with any information, evidence or complaints related to the matter and share it with the designated SIT officers.

articles.title: Gurugram Police Revive Door-To-Door Drives To Identify Illegal Bangladeshi Immigrants
articles.description: A year after a verification drive triggered panic among migrant workers, Gurugram Police are launching a fresh exercise. This time, officials say there will be no detention centres

articles.title: Karnataka minister resigns days after joining DK Shivakumar`s cabinet; clarifies he is still in Congress party
articles.description: Ramalinga Reddy clarified that his decision was limited to his ministerial role and not his party membership, asserting that he has "not resigned" from the party.

articles.title: Indian-origin woman wins Rs 85 crore settlement after 23-year UK divorce battle
articles.description: An Indian-origin woman has won a £6.6 million divorce settlement after a 23-year legal battle in the UK. The ruling ends one of Britain's longest-running divorce disputes involving former solicitor Bhadresh Gohil.

articles.title: After TCS Nashik, ex-Wipro Pune employee alleges forced conversion: What’s going on?
articles.description: A former female employee at Wipro's Pune office has alleged that her ex-supervisors harassed her over religious grounds and tried to forcefully convert her during her tenure with the company from 2019 to 2025. She has filed a police complaint and approached the state’s human rights body against her former colleague. This incident comes months after attempts at forced religious conversion at Tata Consultancy Services' (TCS) Nashik unit

articles.description: DK Shivakumar was sworn in as Karnataka Chief Minister on June 3 along with 13 ministers.

articles.title: NDA finalises Rajya Sabha seat sharing in Andhra Pradesh; TDP to contest 3 seats, Jana Sena gets 1
articles.description: Under the revised seat-sharing arrangement, the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) will contest three of the four Rajya Sabha seats becoming vacant in Andhra Pradesh, while the remaining seat has been allocated to the Jana Sena Party (JSP).

articles.title: Transit visas explained: what they are, when Indians need them, and how to apply
articles.description: Navigating international travel requires understanding transit visa rules, especially for Indian citizens. While some European nations have eased requirements, countries like the US, UK, and Canada maintain strict policies. Knowing the difference between airside and landside transit is crucial to avoid unexpected visa needs and ensure smooth connections.

articles.title: Arunachal CM Khandu brushes aside China's territorial claims, repeated renaming of places: ‘We don’t give a damn'
articles.description: Khandu said that Beijing's claims were nothing new, while stating that China's frequent attempts to rename regions in Arunchal Pradesh were out of “habit”.

articles.title: 'It's possible': Trump hints at meeting Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei amid deal talks
articles.description: In a bold statement, President Trump declared that Iran's military capabilities are severely diminished, stating that the country’s naval and aerial forces have become largely ineffective, leaving its leadership under significant strain. He criticized the media for exaggerating Iran's military strength and issued a warning about potential consequences if any US troops are endangered.

articles.title: 'We are making a lot of money with India': Trump praises PM Modi, backs US-India trade agreement
articles.description: Donald Trump expressed confidence that the two countries would reach a trade agreement while also making clear that the era of one-sided arrangements was over

articles.title: Right to strike: A moment of reckoning
articles.description: Labour law: The ICJ has affirmed the right to strike under ILO Convention 87, renewing debate on India's labour laws. Legal experts say the ruling could reshape collective bargaining, union rights and Industrial Relations Code reforms ahead.
